<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11499495]] Wikidata name: sanni [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''San'i''' (散位) refers to individuals under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] who held only a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} without an associated post in either {{ill|central ministries|ja|京官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/central ministries|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} or {{ill|provincial offices|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}}. They were also called '''{{ill|Sankan|ja|散官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankan|1=prestige title|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5968922}}'''. A similar system of "scattered officials" existed in [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang China]], where civil officials were granted honorary positions such as {{ill|Guanglu Daifu|ja|光禄大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanglu Daifu|1=guanglu dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9400127}} and military officials were given Ranks like {{ill|Zhenjun Da Jiangjun|ja|鎮軍大将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhenjun Da Jiangjun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, these were honorary Ranks conferred on all officials with {{ill|Pin Ranks|ja|品階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pin Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, functioning similarly to Japan’s court Ranks. Other related terms include '''{{ill|Sanji|ja|散事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 散位|1=sanni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499495}}''', referring to lower-Ranking [[:en:Lady-in-waiting|court ladies]] such as {{ill|Nyoshu|ja|女嬬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyoshu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 女孺|1=nyoju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10942756}} and {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}, as well as to provincial minor officials called '''{{ill|Sanshi|ja|散仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.
== Overview ==
Given the limitations on the number of official posts and staff quotas in the Ritsuryō system, it was impossible to assign posts to all officials in accordance with the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]]. This issue was particularly pronounced in Japan, where the systems of {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} (privilege based on lineage) and {{ill|Ishi|ja|位子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi|1=Ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381646}} (court Rank inheritance) created an excess of Ranked individuals, many of whom became San'i.
San'i status encompassed various scenarios, including: - Individuals with court Ranks from the {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} or Ishi systems who lacked official a11ointments. - Those who lost their positions due to {{ill|retirement|ja|致仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/retirement|1=retirement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10493537}} or administrative restructuring. - Those awaiting rea11ointment after Rank promotion. - Individuals temporarily relieved of duties due to illness or mourning. - Newly Ranked persons without a11ointments, including those elevated from {{ill|Hakuchō|ja|白丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchō|qq=}} (commoners) or {{ill|Mubetsu|ja|无位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mubetsu|1=Unranked|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504610}} (Rankless individuals) through evaluations. - Those who gained Ranks through tribute or monetary offerings.
Higher-Ranking San'i (fifth Rank and above) stationed in the capital were assigned to the {{ill|San'i Bureau|ja|散位寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i Bureau|1=Saniryo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499496}}, while those of lower Rank (sixth Rank and below) either served at the San'i Bureau on a rotational basis if residing in the capital or at local {{ill|provincial offices|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}} if based in the provinces. San'i could also receive secondary a11ointments to various offices, including {{ill|Ritsuryō-external institutions|ja|造寺司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō-external institutions|1=zōjishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639742}}, or work under provincial governors as deputies ({{ill|Mokudai|ja|目代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokudai|1=Mokudai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581544}}) or lower-Ranking officials ({{ill|Zaichō Kanjin|ja|在庁官人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaichō Kanjin|1=zaichō kannin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423949}}). To address the growing number of San'i, a fixed quota system was introduced. Those exceeding the quota paid {{ill|Zokurō Coins|ja|続労銭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokurō Coins|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 贖労銭|1=Zokurō Coins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635147}} to substitute for work obligations.
San'i status could also result from inadequate qualifications for a specific role, as prescribed under the {{ill|Senjo-ryō|ja|選叙令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjo-ryō|1=Senjo-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65285114}} (Selection and A11ointment Ordinance). Although not inherently a title for self-identification, it became a significant marker of status for local elites such as {{ill|Gunji|ja|郡司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} or {{ill|Tōne|ja|刀禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōne|1=Tone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396060}}, who used their San'i Rank in documentation to distinguish themselves from commoners and highlight their privileges, including tax exemptions.
== References == * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} 6 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985), ISBN 978-4-642-00505-0. * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Hori Noriko, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja|日本古代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asakura Shoten, 2005), ISBN 978-4-254-53014-8. * Uemura Takashi, "Ancient Official Systems and the Role of 'Shoku'," in *Studies on the Formation of 'Shoku'* (Kōzō Shoin, 2011), ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.

This issue was particularly pronounced in Japan, where the systems of {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} (privilege based on lineage) and {{ill|Ishi|ja|位子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi|1=Ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381646}} (court Rank inheritance) created an excess of Ranked individuals, many of whom became San'i.
San'i status encompassed various scenarios, including: - Individuals with court Ranks from the {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} or Ishi systems who lacked official a11ointments. - Those who lost their positions due to {{ill|retirement|ja|致仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/retirement|1=retirement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10493537}} or administrative restructuring. - Those awaiting rea11ointment after Rank promotion. - Individuals temporarily relieved of duties due to illness or mourning. - Newly Ranked persons without a11ointments, including those elevated from {{ill|Hakuchō|ja|白丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchō|qq=}} (commoners) or {{ill|Mubetsu|ja|无位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mubetsu|1=Unranked|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504610}} (Rankless individuals) through evaluations. - Those who gained Ranks through tribute or monetary offerings.
Higher-Ranking San'i (fifth Rank and above) stationed in the capital were assigned to the {{ill|San'i Bureau|ja|散位寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i Bureau|1=Saniryo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499496}}, while those of lower Rank (sixth Rank and below) either served at the San'i Bureau on a rotational basis if residing in the capital or at local {{ill|provincial offices|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}} if based in the provinces. San'i could also receive secondary a11ointments to various offices, including {{ill|Ritsuryō-external institutions|ja|造寺司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō-external institutions|1=zōjishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639742}}, or work under provincial governors as deputies ({{ill|Mokudai|ja|目代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokudai|1=Mokudai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581544}}) or lower-Ranking officials ({{ill|Zaichō Kanjin|ja|在庁官人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaichō Kanjin|1=zaichō kannin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423949}}). To address the growing number of San'i, a fixed quota system was introduced. Those exceeding the quota paid {{ill|Zokurō Coins|ja|続労銭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokurō Coins|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 贖労銭|1=Zokurō Coins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635147}} to substitute for work obligations.
San'i status could also result from inadequate qualifications for a specific role, as prescribed under the {{ill|Senjo-ryō|ja|選叙令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjo-ryō|1=Senjo-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65285114}} (Selection and A11ointment Ordinance). Although not inherently a title for self-identification, it became a significant marker of status for local elites such as {{ill|Gunji|ja|郡司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} or {{ill|Tōne|ja|刀禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōne|1=Tone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396060}}, who used their San'i Rank in documentation to distinguish themselves from commoners and highlight their privileges, including tax exemptions.
== References == * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} 6 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985), ISBN 978-4-642-00505-0. * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Hori Noriko, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja|日本古代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asakura Shoten, 2005), ISBN 978-4-254-53014-8. * Uemura Takashi, "Ancient Official Systems and the Role of 'Shoku'," in *Studies on the Formation of 'Shoku'* (Kōzō Shoin, 2011), ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.

<!--San'i--><!--San'i--><!--San'i--><!--San'i--><!--San'i--><!--San'i--><!--San'i--><!--San'i--><!--San'i--><!--San'i--> [[Category:Ritsuryō System of Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11499495}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11499495]] Wikidata name: sanni [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''San'i''' (散位) refers to individuals under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] who held only a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} without an associated post in either {{ill|central ministries|ja|京官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/central ministries|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} or {{ill|provincial offices|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}}. They were also called '''{{ill|Sankan|ja|散官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankan|1=prestige title|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5968922}}'''. A similar system of "scattered officials" existed in [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang China]], where civil officials were granted honorary positions such as {{ill|Guanglu Daifu|ja|光禄大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanglu Daifu|1=guanglu dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9400127}} and military officials were given Ranks like {{ill|Zhenjun Da Jiangjun|ja|鎮軍大将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhenjun Da Jiangjun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, these were honorary Ranks conferred on all officials with {{ill|Pin Ranks|ja|品階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pin Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, functioning similarly to Japan’s court Ranks. Other related terms include '''{{ill|Sanji|ja|散事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 散位|1=sanni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499495}}''', referring to lower-Ranking [[:en:Lady-in-waiting|court ladies]] such as {{ill|Nyoshu|ja|女嬬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyoshu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 女孺|1=nyoju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10942756}} and {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}, as well as to provincial minor officials called '''{{ill|Sanshi|ja|散仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.
== Overview ==
Given the limitations on the number of official posts and staff quotas in the Ritsuryō system, it was impossible to assign posts to all officials in accordance with the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]]. This issue was particularly pronounced in Japan, where the systems of {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} (privilege based on lineage) and {{ill|Ishi|ja|位子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi|1=Ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381646}} (court Rank inheritance) created an excess of Ranked individuals, many of whom became San'i.
San'i status encompassed various scenarios, including: - Individuals with court Ranks from the {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} or Ishi systems who lacked official a11ointments. - Those who lost their positions due to {{ill|retirement|ja|致仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/retirement|1=retirement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10493537}} or administrative restructuring. - Those awaiting rea11ointment after Rank promotion. - Individuals temporarily relieved of duties due to illness or mourning. - Newly Ranked persons without a11ointments, including those elevated from {{ill|Hakuchō|ja|白丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchō|qq=}} (commoners) or {{ill|Mubetsu|ja|无位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mubetsu|1=Unranked|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504610}} (Rankless individuals) through evaluations. - Those who gained Ranks through tribute or monetary offerings.
Higher-Ranking San'i (fifth Rank and above) stationed in the capital were assigned to the {{ill|San'i Bureau|ja|散位寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i Bureau|1=Saniryo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499496}}, while those of lower Rank (sixth Rank and below) either served at the San'i Bureau on a rotational basis if residing in the capital or at local {{ill|provincial offices|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}} if based in the provinces. San'i could also receive secondary a11ointments to various offices, including {{ill|Ritsuryō-external institutions|ja|造寺司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō-external institutions|1=zōjishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639742}}, or work under provincial governors as deputies ({{ill|Mokudai|ja|目代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokudai|1=Mokudai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581544}}) or lower-Ranking officials ({{ill|Zaichō Kanjin|ja|在庁官人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaichō Kanjin|1=zaichō kannin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423949}}). To address the growing number of San'i, a fixed quota system was introduced. Those exceeding the quota paid {{ill|Zokurō Coins|ja|続労銭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokurō Coins|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 贖労銭|1=Zokurō Coins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635147}} to substitute for work obligations.
San'i status could also result from inadequate qualifications for a specific role, as prescribed under the {{ill|Senjo-ryō|ja|選叙令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjo-ryō|1=Senjo-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65285114}} (Selection and A11ointment Ordinance). Although not inherently a title for self-identification, it became a significant marker of status for local elites such as {{ill|Gunji|ja|郡司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} or {{ill|Tōne|ja|刀禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōne|1=Tone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396060}}, who used their San'i Rank in documentation to distinguish themselves from commoners and highlight their privileges, including tax exemptions.
== References == * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} 6 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985), ISBN 978-4-642-00505-0. * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Hori Noriko, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja|日本古代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asakura Shoten, 2005), ISBN 978-4-254-53014-8. * Uemura Takashi, "Ancient Official Systems and the Role of 'Shoku'," in *Studies on the Formation of 'Shoku'* (Kōzō Shoin, 2011), ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.

Given the limitations on the number of official posts and staff quotas in the Ritsuryō system, it was impossible to assign posts to all officials in accordance with the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]]. This issue was particularly pronounced in Japan, where the systems of {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} (privilege based on lineage) and {{ill|Ishi|ja|位子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi|1=Ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381646}} (court Rank inheritance) created an excess of Ranked individuals, many of whom became San'i.
San'i status encompassed various scenarios, including: - Individuals with court Ranks from the {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} or Ishi systems who lacked official a11ointments. - Those who lost their positions due to {{ill|retirement|ja|致仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/retirement|1=retirement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10493537}} or administrative restructuring. - Those awaiting rea11ointment after Rank promotion. - Individuals temporarily relieved of duties due to illness or mourning. - Newly Ranked persons without a11ointments, including those elevated from {{ill|Hakuchō|ja|白丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchō|qq=}} (commoners) or {{ill|Mubetsu|ja|无位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mubetsu|1=Unranked|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504610}} (Rankless individuals) through evaluations. - Those who gained Ranks through tribute or monetary offerings.
Higher-Ranking San'i (fifth Rank and above) stationed in the capital were assigned to the {{ill|San'i Bureau|ja|散位寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i Bureau|1=Saniryo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499496}}, while those of lower Rank (sixth Rank and below) either served at the San'i Bureau on a rotational basis if residing in the capital or at local {{ill|provincial offices|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}} if based in the provinces. San'i could also receive secondary a11ointments to various offices, including {{ill|Ritsuryō-external institutions|ja|造寺司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō-external institutions|1=zōjishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639742}}, or work under provincial governors as deputies ({{ill|Mokudai|ja|目代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokudai|1=Mokudai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581544}}) or lower-Ranking officials ({{ill|Zaichō Kanjin|ja|在庁官人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaichō Kanjin|1=zaichō kannin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423949}}). To address the growing number of San'i, a fixed quota system was introduced. Those exceeding the quota paid {{ill|Zokurō Coins|ja|続労銭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokurō Coins|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 贖労銭|1=Zokurō Coins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635147}} to substitute for work obligations.
San'i status could also result from inadequate qualifications for a specific role, as prescribed under the {{ill|Senjo-ryō|ja|選叙令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjo-ryō|1=Senjo-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65285114}} (Selection and A11ointment Ordinance). Although not inherently a title for self-identification, it became a significant marker of status for local elites such as {{ill|Gunji|ja|郡司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} or {{ill|Tōne|ja|刀禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōne|1=Tone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396060}}, who used their San'i Rank in documentation to distinguish themselves from commoners and highlight their privileges, including tax exemptions.
== References == * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} 6 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985), ISBN 978-4-642-00505-0. * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Hori Noriko, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja|日本古代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asakura Shoten, 2005), ISBN 978-4-254-53014-8. * Uemura Takashi, "Ancient Official Systems and the Role of 'Shoku'," in *Studies on the Formation of 'Shoku'* (Kōzō Shoin, 2011), ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11499495]] Wikidata name: sanni [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''San'i''' (散位) refers to individuals under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] who held only a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} without an associated post in either {{ill|central ministries|ja|京官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/central ministries|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} or {{ill|provincial offices|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}}. They were also called '''{{ill|Sankan|ja|散官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankan|1=prestige title|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5968922}}'''. A similar system of "scattered officials" existed in [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang China]], where civil officials were granted honorary positions such as {{ill|Guanglu Daifu|ja|光禄大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanglu Daifu|1=guanglu dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9400127}} and military officials were given Ranks like {{ill|Zhenjun Da Jiangjun|ja|鎮軍大将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhenjun Da Jiangjun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, these were honorary Ranks conferred on all officials with {{ill|Pin Ranks|ja|品階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pin Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, functioning similarly to Japan’s court Ranks. Other related terms include '''{{ill|Sanji|ja|散事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 散位|1=sanni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499495}}''', referring to lower-Ranking [[:en:Lady-in-waiting|court ladies]] such as {{ill|Nyoshu|ja|女嬬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyoshu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 女孺|1=nyoju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10942756}} and {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}, as well as to provincial minor officials called '''{{ill|Sanshi|ja|散仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.
== Overview ==
Given the limitations on the number of official posts and staff quotas in the Ritsuryō system, it was impossible to assign posts to all officials in accordance with the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]]. This issue was particularly pronounced in Japan, where the systems of {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} (privilege based on lineage) and {{ill|Ishi|ja|位子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi|1=Ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381646}} (court Rank inheritance) created an excess of Ranked individuals, many of whom became San'i.
San'i status encompassed various scenarios, including: - Individuals with court Ranks from the {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} or Ishi systems who lacked official a11ointments. - Those who lost their positions due to {{ill|retirement|ja|致仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/retirement|1=retirement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10493537}} or administrative restructuring. - Those awaiting rea11ointment after Rank promotion. - Individuals temporarily relieved of duties due to illness or mourning. - Newly Ranked persons without a11ointments, including those elevated from {{ill|Hakuchō|ja|白丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchō|qq=}} (commoners) or {{ill|Mubetsu|ja|无位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mubetsu|1=Unranked|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504610}} (Rankless individuals) through evaluations. - Those who gained Ranks through tribute or monetary offerings.
Higher-Ranking San'i (fifth Rank and above) stationed in the capital were assigned to the {{ill|San'i Bureau|ja|散位寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i Bureau|1=Saniryo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499496}}, while those of lower Rank (sixth Rank and below) either served at the San'i Bureau on a rotational basis if residing in the capital or at local {{ill|provincial offices|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}} if based in the provinces. San'i could also receive secondary a11ointments to various offices, including {{ill|Ritsuryō-external institutions|ja|造寺司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō-external institutions|1=zōjishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639742}}, or work under provincial governors as deputies ({{ill|Mokudai|ja|目代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokudai|1=Mokudai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581544}}) or lower-Ranking officials ({{ill|Zaichō Kanjin|ja|在庁官人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaichō Kanjin|1=zaichō kannin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423949}}). To address the growing number of San'i, a fixed quota system was introduced. Those exceeding the quota paid {{ill|Zokurō Coins|ja|続労銭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokurō Coins|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 贖労銭|1=Zokurō Coins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635147}} to substitute for work obligations.
San'i status could also result from inadequate qualifications for a specific role, as prescribed under the {{ill|Senjo-ryō|ja|選叙令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjo-ryō|1=Senjo-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65285114}} (Selection and A11ointment Ordinance). Although not inherently a title for self-identification, it became a significant marker of status for local elites such as {{ill|Gunji|ja|郡司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} or {{ill|Tōne|ja|刀禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōne|1=Tone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396060}}, who used their San'i Rank in documentation to distinguish themselves from commoners and highlight their privileges, including tax exemptions.
== References == * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} 6 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985), ISBN 978-4-642-00505-0. * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Hori Noriko, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja|日本古代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asakura Shoten, 2005), ISBN 978-4-254-53014-8. * Uemura Takashi, "Ancient Official Systems and the Role of 'Shoku'," in *Studies on the Formation of 'Shoku'* (Kōzō Shoin, 2011), ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.

== Overview ==
Given the limitations on the number of official posts and staff quotas in the Ritsuryō system, it was impossible to assign posts to all officials in accordance with the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]]. This issue was particularly pronounced in Japan, where the systems of {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} (privilege based on lineage) and {{ill|Ishi|ja|位子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi|1=Ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381646}} (court Rank inheritance) created an excess of Ranked individuals, many of whom became San'i.
San'i status encompassed various scenarios, including: - Individuals with court Ranks from the {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} or Ishi systems who lacked official a11ointments. - Those who lost their positions due to {{ill|retirement|ja|致仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/retirement|1=retirement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10493537}} or administrative restructuring. - Those awaiting rea11ointment after Rank promotion. - Individuals temporarily relieved of duties due to illness or mourning. - Newly Ranked persons without a11ointments, including those elevated from {{ill|Hakuchō|ja|白丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchō|qq=}} (commoners) or {{ill|Mubetsu|ja|无位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mubetsu|1=Unranked|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504610}} (Rankless individuals) through evaluations. - Those who gained Ranks through tribute or monetary offerings.
Higher-Ranking San'i (fifth Rank and above) stationed in the capital were assigned to the {{ill|San'i Bureau|ja|散位寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i Bureau|1=Saniryo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499496}}, while those of lower Rank (sixth Rank and below) either served at the San'i Bureau on a rotational basis if residing in the capital or at local {{ill|provincial offices|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}} if based in the provinces. San'i could also receive secondary a11ointments to various offices, including {{ill|Ritsuryō-external institutions|ja|造寺司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō-external institutions|1=zōjishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639742}}, or work under provincial governors as deputies ({{ill|Mokudai|ja|目代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokudai|1=Mokudai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581544}}) or lower-Ranking officials ({{ill|Zaichō Kanjin|ja|在庁官人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaichō Kanjin|1=zaichō kannin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423949}}). To address the growing number of San'i, a fixed quota system was introduced. Those exceeding the quota paid {{ill|Zokurō Coins|ja|続労銭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokurō Coins|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 贖労銭|1=Zokurō Coins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635147}} to substitute for work obligations.
San'i status could also result from inadequate qualifications for a specific role, as prescribed under the {{ill|Senjo-ryō|ja|選叙令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjo-ryō|1=Senjo-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65285114}} (Selection and A11ointment Ordinance). Although not inherently a title for self-identification, it became a significant marker of status for local elites such as {{ill|Gunji|ja|郡司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} or {{ill|Tōne|ja|刀禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōne|1=Tone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396060}}, who used their San'i Rank in documentation to distinguish themselves from commoners and highlight their privileges, including tax exemptions.
== References == * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} 6 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985), ISBN 978-4-642-00505-0. * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Hori Noriko, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja|日本古代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asakura Shoten, 2005), ISBN 978-4-254-53014-8. * Uemura Takashi, "Ancient Official Systems and the Role of 'Shoku'," in *Studies on the Formation of 'Shoku'* (Kōzō Shoin, 2011), ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11499495]] Wikidata name: sanni [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''San'i''' (散位) refers to individuals under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] who held only a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} without an associated post in either {{ill|central ministries|ja|京官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/central ministries|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} or {{ill|provincial offices|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}}. They were also called '''{{ill|Sankan|ja|散官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankan|1=prestige title|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5968922}}'''. A similar system of "scattered officials" existed in [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang China]], where civil officials were granted honorary positions such as {{ill|Guanglu Daifu|ja|光禄大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanglu Daifu|1=guanglu dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9400127}} and military officials were given Ranks like {{ill|Zhenjun Da Jiangjun|ja|鎮軍大将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhenjun Da Jiangjun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, these were honorary Ranks conferred on all officials with {{ill|Pin Ranks|ja|品階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pin Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, functioning similarly to Japan’s court Ranks. Other related terms include '''{{ill|Sanji|ja|散事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 散位|1=sanni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499495}}''', referring to lower-Ranking [[:en:Lady-in-waiting|court ladies]] such as {{ill|Nyoshu|ja|女嬬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyoshu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 女孺|1=nyoju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10942756}} and {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}, as well as to provincial minor officials called '''{{ill|Sanshi|ja|散仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.
== Overview ==
Given the limitations on the number of official posts and staff quotas in the Ritsuryō system, it was impossible to assign posts to all officials in accordance with the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]]. This issue was particularly pronounced in Japan, where the systems of {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} (privilege based on lineage) and {{ill|Ishi|ja|位子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi|1=Ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381646}} (court Rank inheritance) created an excess of Ranked individuals, many of whom became San'i.
San'i status encompassed various scenarios, including: - Individuals with court Ranks from the {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} or Ishi systems who lacked official a11ointments. - Those who lost their positions due to {{ill|retirement|ja|致仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/retirement|1=retirement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10493537}} or administrative restructuring. - Those awaiting rea11ointment after Rank promotion. - Individuals temporarily relieved of duties due to illness or mourning. - Newly Ranked persons without a11ointments, including those elevated from {{ill|Hakuchō|ja|白丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchō|qq=}} (commoners) or {{ill|Mubetsu|ja|无位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mubetsu|1=Unranked|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504610}} (Rankless individuals) through evaluations. - Those who gained Ranks through tribute or monetary offerings.
Higher-Ranking San'i (fifth Rank and above) stationed in the capital were assigned to the {{ill|San'i Bureau|ja|散位寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i Bureau|1=Saniryo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499496}}, while those of lower Rank (sixth Rank and below) either served at the San'i Bureau on a rotational basis if residing in the capital or at local {{ill|provincial offices|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}} if based in the provinces. San'i could also receive secondary a11ointments to various offices, including {{ill|Ritsuryō-external institutions|ja|造寺司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō-external institutions|1=zōjishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639742}}, or work under provincial governors as deputies ({{ill|Mokudai|ja|目代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokudai|1=Mokudai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581544}}) or lower-Ranking officials ({{ill|Zaichō Kanjin|ja|在庁官人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaichō Kanjin|1=zaichō kannin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423949}}). To address the growing number of San'i, a fixed quota system was introduced. Those exceeding the quota paid {{ill|Zokurō Coins|ja|続労銭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokurō Coins|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 贖労銭|1=Zokurō Coins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635147}} to substitute for work obligations.
San'i status could also result from inadequate qualifications for a specific role, as prescribed under the {{ill|Senjo-ryō|ja|選叙令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjo-ryō|1=Senjo-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65285114}} (Selection and A11ointment Ordinance). Although not inherently a title for self-identification, it became a significant marker of status for local elites such as {{ill|Gunji|ja|郡司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} or {{ill|Tōne|ja|刀禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōne|1=Tone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396060}}, who used their San'i Rank in documentation to distinguish themselves from commoners and highlight their privileges, including tax exemptions.
== References == * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} 6 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985), ISBN 978-4-642-00505-0. * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Hori Noriko, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja|日本古代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asakura Shoten, 2005), ISBN 978-4-254-53014-8. * Uemura Takashi, "Ancient Official Systems and the Role of 'Shoku'," in *Studies on the Formation of 'Shoku'* (Kōzō Shoin, 2011), ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.

Given the limitations on the number of official posts and staff quotas in the Ritsuryō system, it was impossible to assign posts to all officials in accordance with the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]]. This issue was particularly pronounced in Japan, where the systems of {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} (privilege based on lineage) and {{ill|Ishi|ja|位子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi|1=Ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381646}} (court Rank inheritance) created an excess of Ranked individuals, many of whom became San'i.
San'i status encompassed various scenarios, including: - Individuals with court Ranks from the {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} or Ishi systems who lacked official a11ointments. - Those who lost their positions due to {{ill|retirement|ja|致仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/retirement|1=retirement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10493537}} or administrative restructuring. - Those awaiting rea11ointment after Rank promotion. - Individuals temporarily relieved of duties due to illness or mourning. - Newly Ranked persons without a11ointments, including those elevated from {{ill|Hakuchō|ja|白丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchō|qq=}} (commoners) or {{ill|Mubetsu|ja|无位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mubetsu|1=Unranked|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504610}} (Rankless individuals) through evaluations. - Those who gained Ranks through tribute or monetary offerings.
Higher-Ranking San'i (fifth Rank and above) stationed in the capital were assigned to the {{ill|San'i Bureau|ja|散位寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i Bureau|1=Saniryo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499496}}, while those of lower Rank (sixth Rank and below) either served at the San'i Bureau on a rotational basis if residing in the capital or at local {{ill|provincial offices|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}} if based in the provinces. San'i could also receive secondary a11ointments to various offices, including {{ill|Ritsuryō-external institutions|ja|造寺司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō-external institutions|1=zōjishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639742}}, or work under provincial governors as deputies ({{ill|Mokudai|ja|目代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokudai|1=Mokudai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581544}}) or lower-Ranking officials ({{ill|Zaichō Kanjin|ja|在庁官人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaichō Kanjin|1=zaichō kannin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423949}}). To address the growing number of San'i, a fixed quota system was introduced. Those exceeding the quota paid {{ill|Zokurō Coins|ja|続労銭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokurō Coins|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 贖労銭|1=Zokurō Coins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635147}} to substitute for work obligations.
San'i status could also result from inadequate qualifications for a specific role, as prescribed under the {{ill|Senjo-ryō|ja|選叙令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjo-ryō|1=Senjo-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65285114}} (Selection and A11ointment Ordinance). Although not inherently a title for self-identification, it became a significant marker of status for local elites such as {{ill|Gunji|ja|郡司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} or {{ill|Tōne|ja|刀禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōne|1=Tone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396060}}, who used their San'i Rank in documentation to distinguish themselves from commoners and highlight their privileges, including tax exemptions.
== References == * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} 6 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985), ISBN 978-4-642-00505-0. * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Hori Noriko, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja|日本古代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asakura Shoten, 2005), ISBN 978-4-254-53014-8. * Uemura Takashi, "Ancient Official Systems and the Role of 'Shoku'," in *Studies on the Formation of 'Shoku'* (Kōzō Shoin, 2011), ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11499495]] Wikidata name: sanni [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''San'i''' (散位) refers to individuals under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] who held only a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} without an associated post in either {{ill|central ministries|ja|京官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/central ministries|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} or {{ill|provincial offices|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}}. They were also called '''{{ill|Sankan|ja|散官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankan|1=prestige title|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5968922}}'''. A similar system of "scattered officials" existed in [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang China]], where civil officials were granted honorary positions such as {{ill|Guanglu Daifu|ja|光禄大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanglu Daifu|1=guanglu dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9400127}} and military officials were given Ranks like {{ill|Zhenjun Da Jiangjun|ja|鎮軍大将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhenjun Da Jiangjun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, these were honorary Ranks conferred on all officials with {{ill|Pin Ranks|ja|品階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pin Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, functioning similarly to Japan’s court Ranks. Other related terms include '''{{ill|Sanji|ja|散事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 散位|1=sanni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499495}}''', referring to lower-Ranking [[:en:Lady-in-waiting|court ladies]] such as {{ill|Nyoshu|ja|女嬬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyoshu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 女孺|1=nyoju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10942756}} and {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}, as well as to provincial minor officials called '''{{ill|Sanshi|ja|散仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.
== Overview ==
Given the limitations on the number of official posts and staff quotas in the Ritsuryō system, it was impossible to assign posts to all officials in accordance with the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]]. This issue was particularly pronounced in Japan, where the systems of {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} (privilege based on lineage) and {{ill|Ishi|ja|位子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi|1=Ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381646}} (court Rank inheritance) created an excess of Ranked individuals, many of whom became San'i.
San'i status encompassed various scenarios, including: - Individuals with court Ranks from the {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} or Ishi systems who lacked official a11ointments. - Those who lost their positions due to {{ill|retirement|ja|致仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/retirement|1=retirement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10493537}} or administrative restructuring. - Those awaiting rea11ointment after Rank promotion. - Individuals temporarily relieved of duties due to illness or mourning. - Newly Ranked persons without a11ointments, including those elevated from {{ill|Hakuchō|ja|白丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchō|qq=}} (commoners) or {{ill|Mubetsu|ja|无位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mubetsu|1=Unranked|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504610}} (Rankless individuals) through evaluations. - Those who gained Ranks through tribute or monetary offerings.
Higher-Ranking San'i (fifth Rank and above) stationed in the capital were assigned to the {{ill|San'i Bureau|ja|散位寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i Bureau|1=Saniryo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499496}}, while those of lower Rank (sixth Rank and below) either served at the San'i Bureau on a rotational basis if residing in the capital or at local {{ill|provincial offices|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}} if based in the provinces. San'i could also receive secondary a11ointments to various offices, including {{ill|Ritsuryō-external institutions|ja|造寺司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō-external institutions|1=zōjishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639742}}, or work under provincial governors as deputies ({{ill|Mokudai|ja|目代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokudai|1=Mokudai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581544}}) or lower-Ranking officials ({{ill|Zaichō Kanjin|ja|在庁官人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaichō Kanjin|1=zaichō kannin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423949}}). To address the growing number of San'i, a fixed quota system was introduced. Those exceeding the quota paid {{ill|Zokurō Coins|ja|続労銭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokurō Coins|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 贖労銭|1=Zokurō Coins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635147}} to substitute for work obligations.
San'i status could also result from inadequate qualifications for a specific role, as prescribed under the {{ill|Senjo-ryō|ja|選叙令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjo-ryō|1=Senjo-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65285114}} (Selection and A11ointment Ordinance). Although not inherently a title for self-identification, it became a significant marker of status for local elites such as {{ill|Gunji|ja|郡司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} or {{ill|Tōne|ja|刀禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōne|1=Tone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396060}}, who used their San'i Rank in documentation to distinguish themselves from commoners and highlight their privileges, including tax exemptions.
== References == * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} 6 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985), ISBN 978-4-642-00505-0. * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Hori Noriko, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja|日本古代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asakura Shoten, 2005), ISBN 978-4-254-53014-8. * Uemura Takashi, "Ancient Official Systems and the Role of 'Shoku'," in *Studies on the Formation of 'Shoku'* (Kōzō Shoin, 2011), ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.

Given the limitations on the number of official posts and staff quotas in the Ritsuryō system, it was impossible to assign posts to all officials in accordance with the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]]. This issue was particularly pronounced in Japan, where the systems of {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} (privilege based on lineage) and {{ill|Ishi|ja|位子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi|1=Ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381646}} (court Rank inheritance) created an excess of Ranked individuals, many of whom became San'i.
San'i status encompassed various scenarios, including: - Individuals with court Ranks from the {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} or Ishi systems who lacked official a11ointments. - Those who lost their positions due to {{ill|retirement|ja|致仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/retirement|1=retirement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10493537}} or administrative restructuring. - Those awaiting rea11ointment after Rank promotion. - Individuals temporarily relieved of duties due to illness or mourning. - Newly Ranked persons without a11ointments, including those elevated from {{ill|Hakuchō|ja|白丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchō|qq=}} (commoners) or {{ill|Mubetsu|ja|无位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mubetsu|1=Unranked|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504610}} (Rankless individuals) through evaluations. - Those who gained Ranks through tribute or monetary offerings.
Higher-Ranking San'i (fifth Rank and above) stationed in the capital were assigned to the {{ill|San'i Bureau|ja|散位寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i Bureau|1=Saniryo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499496}}, while those of lower Rank (sixth Rank and below) either served at the San'i Bureau on a rotational basis if residing in the capital or at local {{ill|provincial offices|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}} if based in the provinces. San'i could also receive secondary a11ointments to various offices, including {{ill|Ritsuryō-external institutions|ja|造寺司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō-external institutions|1=zōjishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639742}}, or work under provincial governors as deputies ({{ill|Mokudai|ja|目代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokudai|1=Mokudai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581544}}) or lower-Ranking officials ({{ill|Zaichō Kanjin|ja|在庁官人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaichō Kanjin|1=zaichō kannin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423949}}). To address the growing number of San'i, a fixed quota system was introduced. Those exceeding the quota paid {{ill|Zokurō Coins|ja|続労銭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokurō Coins|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 贖労銭|1=Zokurō Coins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635147}} to substitute for work obligations.
San'i status could also result from inadequate qualifications for a specific role, as prescribed under the {{ill|Senjo-ryō|ja|選叙令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjo-ryō|1=Senjo-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65285114}} (Selection and A11ointment Ordinance). Although not inherently a title for self-identification, it became a significant marker of status for local elites such as {{ill|Gunji|ja|郡司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} or {{ill|Tōne|ja|刀禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōne|1=Tone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396060}}, who used their San'i Rank in documentation to distinguish themselves from commoners and highlight their privileges, including tax exemptions.
== References == * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} 6 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985), ISBN 978-4-642-00505-0. * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Hori Noriko, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja|日本古代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asakura Shoten, 2005), ISBN 978-4-254-53014-8. * Uemura Takashi, "Ancient Official Systems and the Role of 'Shoku'," in *Studies on the Formation of 'Shoku'* (Kōzō Shoin, 2011), ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.
{{translated page|ja|散位|version=92067585|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11499495}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Ritsuryō System of Japan]] [[Category:San'i]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11499495]] Wikidata name: sanni [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''San'i''' (散位) refers to individuals under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]] who held only a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} without an associated post in either {{ill|central ministries|ja|京官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/central ministries|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} or {{ill|provincial offices|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}}. They were also called '''{{ill|Sankan|ja|散官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankan|1=prestige title|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5968922}}'''. A similar system of "scattered officials" existed in [[:en:Tang dynasty|Tang China]], where civil officials were granted honorary positions such as {{ill|Guanglu Daifu|ja|光禄大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanglu Daifu|1=guanglu dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9400127}} and military officials were given Ranks like {{ill|Zhenjun Da Jiangjun|ja|鎮軍大将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhenjun Da Jiangjun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, these were honorary Ranks conferred on all officials with {{ill|Pin Ranks|ja|品階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pin Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, functioning similarly to Japan’s court Ranks. Other related terms include '''{{ill|Sanji|ja|散事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 散位|1=sanni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499495}}''', referring to lower-Ranking [[:en:Lady-in-waiting|court ladies]] such as {{ill|Nyoshu|ja|女嬬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyoshu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 女孺|1=nyoju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10942756}} and {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}, as well as to provincial minor officials called '''{{ill|Sanshi|ja|散仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.
== Overview ==
Given the limitations on the number of official posts and staff quotas in the Ritsuryō system, it was impossible to assign posts to all officials in accordance with the [[:en:List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|Kan'i sōtō system]]. This issue was particularly pronounced in Japan, where the systems of {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} (privilege based on lineage) and {{ill|Ishi|ja|位子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi|1=Ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381646}} (court Rank inheritance) created an excess of Ranked individuals, many of whom became San'i.
San'i status encompassed various scenarios, including: - Individuals with court Ranks from the {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} or Ishi systems who lacked official a11ointments. - Those who lost their positions due to {{ill|retirement|ja|致仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/retirement|1=retirement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10493537}} or administrative restructuring. - Those awaiting rea11ointment after Rank promotion. - Individuals temporarily relieved of duties due to illness or mourning. - Newly Ranked persons without a11ointments, including those elevated from {{ill|Hakuchō|ja|白丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchō|qq=}} (commoners) or {{ill|Mubetsu|ja|无位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mubetsu|1=Unranked|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504610}} (Rankless individuals) through evaluations. - Those who gained Ranks through tribute or monetary offerings.
Higher-Ranking San'i (fifth Rank and above) stationed in the capital were assigned to the {{ill|San'i Bureau|ja|散位寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i Bureau|1=Saniryo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499496}}, while those of lower Rank (sixth Rank and below) either served at the San'i Bureau on a rotational basis if residing in the capital or at local {{ill|provincial offices|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}} if based in the provinces. San'i could also receive secondary a11ointments to various offices, including {{ill|Ritsuryō-external institutions|ja|造寺司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō-external institutions|1=zōjishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639742}}, or work under provincial governors as deputies ({{ill|Mokudai|ja|目代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokudai|1=Mokudai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581544}}) or lower-Ranking officials ({{ill|Zaichō Kanjin|ja|在庁官人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaichō Kanjin|1=zaichō kannin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423949}}). To address the growing number of San'i, a fixed quota system was introduced. Those exceeding the quota paid {{ill|Zokurō Coins|ja|続労銭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokurō Coins|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 贖労銭|1=Zokurō Coins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635147}} to substitute for work obligations.
San'i status could also result from inadequate qualifications for a specific role, as prescribed under the {{ill|Senjo-ryō|ja|選叙令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjo-ryō|1=Senjo-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65285114}} (Selection and A11ointment Ordinance). Although not inherently a title for self-identification, it became a significant marker of status for local elites such as {{ill|Gunji|ja|郡司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} or {{ill|Tōne|ja|刀禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōne|1=Tone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396060}}, who used their San'i Rank in documentation to distinguish themselves from commoners and highlight their privileges, including tax exemptions.
== References == * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} 6 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985), ISBN 978-4-642-00505-0. * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Hori Noriko, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja|日本古代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asakura Shoten, 2005), ISBN 978-4-254-53014-8. * Uemura Takashi, "Ancient Official Systems and the Role of 'Shoku'," in *Studies on the Formation of 'Shoku'* (Kōzō Shoin, 2011), ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.

== Overview ==
Given the limitations on the number of official posts and staff quotas in the Ritsuryō system, it was impossible to assign posts to all officials in accordance with the {{ill|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|WD=Q11452077|lt=Kan'i sōtō system}}. This issue was particularly pronounced in Japan, where the systems of {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} (privilege based on lineage) and {{ill|Ishi|ja|位子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi|1=Ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381646}} (court Rank inheritance) created an excess of Ranked individuals, many of whom became San'i.
San'i status encompassed various scenarios, including: - Individuals with court Ranks from the {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} or Ishi systems who lacked official a11ointments. - Those who lost their positions due to {{ill|retirement|ja|致仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/retirement|1=retirement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10493537}} or administrative restructuring. - Those awaiting rea11ointment after Rank promotion. - Individuals temporarily relieved of duties due to illness or mourning. - Newly Ranked persons without a11ointments, including those elevated from {{ill|Hakuchō|ja|白丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchō|qq=}} (commoners) or {{ill|Mubetsu|ja|无位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mubetsu|1=Unranked|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504610}} (Rankless individuals) through evaluations. - Those who gained Ranks through tribute or monetary offerings.
Higher-Ranking San'i (fifth Rank and above) stationed in the capital were assigned to the {{ill|San'i Bureau|ja|散位寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i Bureau|1=Saniryo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499496}}, while those of lower Rank (sixth Rank and below) either served at the San'i Bureau on a rotational basis if residing in the capital or at local {{ill|provincial offices|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}} if based in the provinces. San'i could also receive secondary a11ointments to various offices, including {{ill|Ritsuryō-external institutions|ja|造寺司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō-external institutions|1=zōjishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639742}}, or work under provincial governors as deputies ({{ill|Mokudai|ja|目代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokudai|1=Mokudai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581544}}) or lower-Ranking officials ({{ill|Zaichō Kanjin|ja|在庁官人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaichō Kanjin|1=zaichō kannin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423949}}). To address the growing number of San'i, a fixed quota system was introduced. Those exceeding the quota paid {{ill|Zokurō Coins|ja|続労銭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokurō Coins|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 贖労銭|1=Zokurō Coins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635147}} to substitute for work obligations.
San'i status could also result from inadequate qualifications for a specific role, as prescribed under the {{ill|Senjo-ryō|ja|選叙令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjo-ryō|1=Senjo-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65285114}} (Selection and A11ointment Ordinance). Although not inherently a title for self-identification, it became a significant marker of status for local elites such as {{ill|Gunji|ja|郡司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} or {{ill|Tōne|ja|刀禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōne|1=Tone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396060}}, who used their San'i Rank in documentation to distinguish themselves from commoners and highlight their privileges, including tax exemptions.
== References == * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} 6 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985), ISBN 978-4-642-00505-0. * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Hori Noriko, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja|日本古代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asakura Shoten, 2005), ISBN 978-4-254-53014-8. * Uemura Takashi, "Ancient Official Systems and the Role of 'Shoku'," in *Studies on the Formation of 'Shoku'* (Kōzō Shoin, 2011), ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.
{{translated page|ja|散位|version=92067585|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:散位]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11499495]] Wikidata name: sanni [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''San'i''' (散位) refers to individuals under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} who held only a {{ill|court Rank|ja|位階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=court rank in Japan|WD=Q99196082}} without an associated post in either {{ill|central ministries|ja|京官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/central ministries|1=Central officials|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10883145}} or {{ill|provincial offices|ja|外官|1=Gaikan|lt=Local officials (Gaikan)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|13=User:Immanuelle/Gaikan|qq=}}. They were also called '''{{ill|Sankan|ja|散官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankan|1=prestige title|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q5968922}}'''. A similar system of "scattered officials" existed in {{ill|Tang dynasty|en|Tang dynasty|WD=Q9683|lt=Tang China}}, where civil officials were granted honorary positions such as {{ill|Guanglu Daifu|ja|光禄大夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guanglu Daifu|1=guanglu dafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9400127}} and military officials were given Ranks like {{ill|Zhenjun Da Jiangjun|ja|鎮軍大将軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhenjun Da Jiangjun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. However, these were honorary Ranks conferred on all officials with {{ill|Pin Ranks|ja|品階|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pin Ranks|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, functioning similarly to Japan’s court Ranks. Other related terms include '''{{ill|Sanji|ja|散事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 散位|1=sanni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499495}}''', referring to lower-Ranking {{ill|Lady-in-waiting|en|Lady-in-waiting|WD=Q715222|lt=court ladies}} such as {{ill|Nyoshu|ja|女嬬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyoshu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 女孺|1=nyoju|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10942756}} and {{ill|Uneme|ja|采女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uneme|qq=}}, as well as to provincial minor officials called '''{{ill|Sanshi|ja|散仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''.
== Overview ==
Given the limitations on the number of official posts and staff quotas in the Ritsuryō system, it was impossible to assign posts to all officials in accordance with the {{ill|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|en|List of Japanese court ranks, positions and hereditary titles|WD=Q11452077|lt=Kan'i sōtō system}}. This issue was particularly pronounced in Japan, where the systems of {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} (privilege based on lineage) and {{ill|Ishi|ja|位子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi|1=Ishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381646}} (court Rank inheritance) created an excess of Ranked individuals, many of whom became San'i.
San'i status encompassed various scenarios, including: - Individuals with court Ranks from the {{ill|Onshi|ja|蔭子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Onshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蔭位}} or Ishi systems who lacked official a11ointments. - Those who lost their positions due to {{ill|retirement|ja|致仕|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/retirement|1=retirement|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10493537}} or administrative restructuring. - Those awaiting rea11ointment after Rank promotion. - Individuals temporarily relieved of duties due to illness or mourning. - Newly Ranked persons without a11ointments, including those elevated from {{ill|Hakuchō|ja|白丁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchō|qq=}} (commoners) or {{ill|Mubetsu|ja|无位|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mubetsu|1=Unranked|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504610}} (Rankless individuals) through evaluations. - Those who gained Ranks through tribute or monetary offerings.
Higher-Ranking San'i (fifth Rank and above) stationed in the capital were assigned to the {{ill|San'i Bureau|ja|散位寮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/San'i Bureau|1=Saniryo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11499496}}, while those of lower Rank (sixth Rank and below) either served at the San'i Bureau on a rotational basis if residing in the capital or at local {{ill|provincial offices|ja|国衙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/provincial offices|1=kokuga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11421342}} if based in the provinces. San'i could also receive secondary a11ointments to various offices, including {{ill|Ritsuryō-external institutions|ja|造寺司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō-external institutions|1=zōjishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639742}}, or work under provincial governors as deputies ({{ill|Mokudai|ja|目代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mokudai|1=Mokudai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11581544}}) or lower-Ranking officials ({{ill|Zaichō Kanjin|ja|在庁官人|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zaichō Kanjin|1=zaichō kannin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423949}}). To address the growing number of San'i, a fixed quota system was introduced. Those exceeding the quota paid {{ill|Zokurō Coins|ja|続労銭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zokurō Coins|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 贖労銭|1=Zokurō Coins|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11635147}} to substitute for work obligations.
San'i status could also result from inadequate qualifications for a specific role, as prescribed under the {{ill|Senjo-ryō|ja|選叙令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senjo-ryō|1=Senjo-ryō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65285114}} (Selection and A11ointment Ordinance). Although not inherently a title for self-identification, it became a significant marker of status for local elites such as {{ill|Gunji|ja|郡司|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunji|qq=}} or {{ill|Tōne|ja|刀禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōne|1=Tone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11396060}}, who used their San'i Rank in documentation to distinguish themselves from commoners and highlight their privileges, including tax exemptions.
== References == * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}} 6 (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1985), ISBN 978-4-642-00505-0. * Yamada Hideo, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihonshi Daijiten|ja|日本史大事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihonshi Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} 3 (Heibonsha, 1993), ISBN 978-4-582-13103-1. * Hori Noriko, "San'i," in {{ill|Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja|日本古代史事典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kodai-shi Jiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Asakura Shoten, 2005), ISBN 978-4-254-53014-8. * Uemura Takashi, "Ancient Official Systems and the Role of 'Shoku'," in *Studies on the Formation of 'Shoku'* (Kōzō Shoin, 2011), ISBN 978-4-7517-4360-7.
